parsed_review.summary: CP00-743 (RP24.P06.M05.S07-S16, risk A, 10 units, Permission And Audit Binding) is a descriptor-only Korean Legal Depth pack and its pack-level descriptor-verification claim is fully supported by independent read-only inspection. Implementation is consistent and complete: KOREAN_LEGAL_CP743_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:366) and KOREAN_LEGAL_CP743_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:3302) match the baseline distribution {implementation:3, security_audit:2, ui:4, claude_review:1}=10 and the 10 required section rows; the descriptor, case set, Hermes evidence packet, Claude review packet, and closeout handoff (service.js:96-97,166-168) chain from CP742 and are re-exported via index.js `export *`. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced structurally: KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is spread into every result, every case-set row carries descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false and excludes raw/legal/credential/client payloads, and validateDescriptor (validators.js:8) rejects any runtime opening, permission-decision detail, audit-event body, or cross-tenant access. Authority boundary is correct: descriptor asserts claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-744), and local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; Claude is a read-only non-final reviewer. Hermes is used only for a deterministic evidence packet (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false), not product runtime. Tests (model.test.js:51-53) assert coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and latest contract projection; the rp24 contract validator re-derives the contract from source and asserts zero drift, all referenced npm scripts exist, and command-evidence shows the 10 required validation commands plus git diff --check at exit 0 with a passing auth preflight. The manifest correctly holds production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false/in_progress pending this review, and the pack keeps HWPX, court filing, litigation, corporate document, Korean clause authority, Hermes runtime, and product-state surfaces closed, claiming neither runtime nor enterprise trust. No P0-P3 findings; pack is ready to close at the descriptor layer.
